claims.subject: Vinod Krishnan
claims.evidence: i own pos self-service end to end. the kiosk product. that means roadmap, prioritization with eng, partnership with the hardware vendor, go-to-market with rajesh's team, and field feedback from the merchants who actually have the kiosks.
claims.object_value: POS Self-Service (kiosk product), end to end

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: we launched pos self-service in 2025.

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: as of last week we have roughly three hundred forty active kiosks across sea.
claims.object_value: roughly 340

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: we are about forty-three percent under plan.
claims.object_value: approximately 43% under plan

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: the kiosks have a three-month lead time from our manufacturing partner.
claims.object_value: three months

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: when we placed orders in q4 expecting a strong q1 we underestimated the q1 demand from a couple of larger merchants and we ran out of units in february.
claims.object_value: ran out of units in February 2026 due to underestimated Q1 demand

claims.object_value: hardware partner sync and deployment review with field ops

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: as of last week we have roughly three hundred forty active kiosks across sea.
claims.object_value: SEA (Southeast Asia)

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: pos self-service is the kiosk, the customer takes the payment themselves.
claims.object_value: kiosk where the customer takes the payment themselves

claims.subject: HelixPay POS
claims.evidence: pos is the card reader plus app, the merchant takes the payment.
claims.object_value: card reader plus app where the merchant takes the payment

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: there is real market confusion between "helixpay pos" and "helixpay pos self-service". they're different products… but the names are basically the same and even our internal sales reps mix them up.
claims.object_value: internal and market confusion with HelixPay POS product name

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: i had a sales rep last month forward me an opportunity tagged as pos self-service that was actually a pos deal. and the inverse happens too — merchants signing for pos and then asking why their kiosk isn't shipping.
claims.object_value: sales reps mis-tagging opportunities and merchants signing wrong product

claims.subject: Vinod Krishnan
claims.evidence: i want to merge the marketing landing pages and run them as one product family with two skus or modes underneath.
claims.object_value: merge marketing landing pages into one product family with two SKUs/modes

claims.subject: Rajesh
claims.evidence: rajesh wants to keep them separate because pos self-service has a different buyer persona — bigger merchants, more procurement-driven, longer cycle — and his data says the messaging needs to be different.
claims.object_value: keep POS and POS Self-Service pages separate due to different buyer persona

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: pos self-service has a different buyer persona — bigger merchants, more procurement-driven, longer cycle
claims.object_value: bigger merchants, more procurement-driven, longer sales cycle

claims.subject: POS Self-Service
claims.evidence: there is real market confusion between "helixpay pos" and "helixpay pos self-service"… even our internal sales reps mix them up.
claims.object_value: market and internal naming confusion between POS and POS Self-Service
